reasoning: 18 consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200 is an extremely rare feat that hasn't been accomplished in the last 30 years. Recent mega-hits like "The Tortured Poets Department" (15 consecutive weeks) and "One Thing at a Time" (12 consecutive weeks) failed to reach the 18-week mark. The streaming era makes it incredibly difficult to prevent being temporarily unseated by new major releases over a 4+ month period. Given the base rate is near 0% for the modern era, the probability is set to the minimum reasonable floor (0.05).
